choiceresponse.label:
Two patients differ only as follows:
Patient A: thal_2 ≤ 0.5, oldpeak −0.151
Patient B: thal_2 0.5, age ≤ 0.019 Which statements are true? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: The two patients follow different paths in the tree.
The model uses different features to explain each patient’s prediction.
The tree combines features additively, like logistic regression.
The patients get the same prediction.
Both patients are evaluated using the same sequence of rules.

div.p: Decision trees explain predictions by routing patients through explicit sequences of conditional rules that end at specific leaf nodes. In this tree, Patient A and Patient B diverge at the root split on thal_2 and are evaluated using different features thereafter (oldpeak for Patient A and age for Patient B). Because they follow different paths, they reach different leaf nodes. In this case, those leaf nodes predict different classes, which is why the two patients receive different final predictions (Patient A ends in up in one of the orange leaf nodes (no disease), while Patient B in one of the blue leaf nodes (disease)). This illustrates a key interpretability property of decision trees: both the prediction and the explanation depend on the path taken, and different patients may be classified using entirely different reasoning structures.
